author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John i. 48,’When thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John i. 48,’When thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What we have heard said by the Lord Jesus Christ to Nathanael, if we understand it aright, does not concern him only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For our Lord Jesus saw the whole human race under the fig-tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in this place it is understood that by the fig-tree He signified sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not that it always signifies this, but as I have said in this place, in that fitness of significancy, in which ye know that the first man, when he sinned, covered himself with fig leaves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For with these leaves they covered their nakedness when they blushed for their sin; and what God had made them for members, they made for themselves occasions of shame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they had no need to blush for the work of God; but the cause of sin preceded shame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If iniquity had not gone before, nakedness would never have been put to the blush.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “they were naked, and were not ashamed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they had committed nothing to be ashamed for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why have I said all this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That we may understand that by the fig-tree sin is signified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is, “when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou wast under sin, I saw thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And Nathanael looking back upon what had occurred, remembered that he had been under a fig-tree, where Christ was not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was not there, that is, by His Bodily Presence; but by His knowledge in the Spirit where is He not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because he knew that he was under the fig-tree alone, where the Lord Christ was not; when He said to him, “When thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee;” he both acknowledged the Divinity in Him, and cried out, “Thou art the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord said, “Because I said unto thee, I saw thee when thou wast under the fig-tree, marvellest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What are these greater things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He said, “Ye shall see heaven open, and the Angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us call to mind the old story written in the sacred Book.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I mean in Genesis.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When Jacob slept at a certain place, he put a stone at his head; and in his sleep he saw a ladder reaching from earth even unto heaven; and the Lord was resting upon it; and Angels were ascending and descending by it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This did Jacob see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man’s dream would not have been recorded, had not some great mystery been figured in it, had not some great prophecy been to be understood in that vision.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly, Jacob himself, because he understood what he had seen, placed a stone there, and anointed it with oil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now ye recognise the anointing; recognise The Anointed also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He is “the Stone which the builders rejected; He was made the Head of the corner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is the Stone of which Himself said, “Whosoever shall stumble against This Stone shall be shaken; but on whomsoever That Stone shall fall, It will crush him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is stumbled against as It lies on the earth; but It will fall on him, when He shall come from on high to judge the quick and dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Woe to the Jews, for that when Christ lay low in His humility, they stumbled against Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “This Man,” say they, “is not of God, because He breaketh the sabbath day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If He be the Son of God, let Him come down from the cross.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Madman, the Stone lies on the ground, and so thou deridest It.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But since thou dost deride It, thou art blind; since thou art blind, thou stumblest; since thou stumblest, thou art shaken; since thou hast been shaken by It as It now lies on the ground, hereafter shall thou be crushed by It as It fails from above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore Jacob anointed the stone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did he make an idol of it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He showed a meaning in it, but did not adore it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now then give ear, attend to this Nathanael, by the occasion of whom the Lord Jesus hath been pleased to explain to us Jacob’s vision.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye that are well instructed in the school of Christ, know that this Jacob is Israel too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are two names; for they are one man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His first name Jacob, which is by interpretation supplanter, he received when he was born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when those twins were born, his brother Esau was born first; and the hand of the younger was found on the elder’s foot.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He held his brother’s foot who preceded him in his birth, and himself came after.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because of this occurrence, because he held his brother’s heel, he was called Jacob, that is, Supplanter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And afterwards, when he was returning from Mesopotamia, the Angel wrestled with him in the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What comparison can there be between an Angel’s and a man’s strength?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore it is a mystery, a sacrament, a prophecy, a figure; let us therefore understand it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For consider the manner of the struggle too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: While he wrestleth, Jacob prevailed against the Angel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some high meaning is here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when the man had prevailed against the Angel, he kept hold of Him; yes, the man kept hold of Him whom he had conquered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And said to Him, “I will not let Thee go, except Thou bless me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the conqueror was blessed by the Conquered, Christ was figured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then that Angel, who is understood to be the Lord Jesus, saith to Jacob, “Thou shalt not be any more called Jacob, but Israel shall thy name be,” which is by interpretation, “Seeing God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After this He touched the sinew of his thigh, the broad part, that is, of the thigh, and it dried up; and Jacob became lame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such was He who was conquered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So great power had this Conquered One, as to touch the thigh, and make lame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was then with His Own will that He was conquered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He “had power to lay down” His strength, “and He had power to take It up.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is not angry at being conquered, for He is not angry at being crucified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He even blessed him, saying, “Thou shalt not be called Jacob, but Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then the “supplanter” was made “the seer of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He touched, as I have said, his thigh, and made him lame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe in Jacob the people of the Jews, those thousands who followed and went before the Lord’s beast, who in concert with the Apostles worshipped the Lord, and cried out, “Hosanna to the Son of David, Blessed is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold Jacob blessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has continued lame until now in them who are at this day Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the broad part of the thigh signifies the multitude of increase.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of whom the Psalm, when it prophesied that the Nations should believe, speaketh, saying, “A people whom I have not known, hath served Me; by the hearing of the ear it hath obeyed Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I was not there, and I was heard; here I was, and I was killed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A people whom I have not known, hath served Me; by the hearing of the ear it hath obeyed Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And it goes on, “The strange children have lied unto Me;” concerning the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The strange children have lied unto Me, the strange children have faded away and have halted from their paths.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have pointed out Jacob to you, Jacob blessed and Jacob lame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as arising out of this occasion, this must not be passed over, which may haply of itself perplex some of you; with what design is it, that when this Jacob’s grandfather Abraham’s name was changed (for he too was first called Abram, and God changed his name, and said, “Thou shall not be called Abram, but Abraham”); from that time he was not called Abram.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Search in the Scriptures, and you will see that before he received another name, he was called only Abram; after he received it, he was called only Abraham.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this Jacob, when he received another name, heard the same words, “Thou shalt not be called Jacob, but Israel shalt thou be called.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Search the Scriptures, and see how that he was always called both, both Jacob and Israel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Abram after he had received another name, was called only Abraham.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Jacob after he had received another name, was called both Jacob and Israel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The name of Abraham was to be developed in this world; for here he was made the father of many nations, whence he received his name.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the name of Israel relates to another world, where we shall see God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the people of God, the Christian people in this present time, is both Jacob and Israel, Jacob in fact, Israel in hope.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the younger people is called the Supplanter of its brother the elder people.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! have we supplanted the Jews?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No, but we are said to be their supplanters, for that for our sakes they were supplanted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If they had not been blinded, Christ would not have been crucified; His precious Blood would not have been shed; if that Blood had not been shed, the world would not have been redeemed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then their blindness hath profited us, therefore hath the elder brother been supplanted by the younger, and the younger is called the Supplanter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how long shall this be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The time will come, the end of the world will come, and all Israel shall believe; not they who now are, but their children who shall then be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For these present walking in their own ways, will go to their own place, will pass on to everlasting damnation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when they shall have been made all one people, that shall come to pass which we sing, “I shall be satisfied when Thy glory shall be manifested.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the promise which is made to us, that we “see face to face,” shall come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Now we see through a glass darkly,” and “in part;” but when both people, now purified, now raised again, now crowned, now changed into an immortal form, and into everlasting incorruption, shall see God face to face, and Jacob shall be no more, but there shall be Israel only; then shall the Lord see him in the person of this holy Nathanael, and shall say, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When thou dost hear, “Behold an Israelite indeed;” let Israel come into thy mind; when Israel shall come into thy mind, let his dream come into thy mind, in which he saw a ladder from earth even to heaven, the Lord standing upon it, the Angels of God ascending and descending.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This dream did Jacob see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But after this he was called Israel; that is, some little time after as he came from Mesopotamia, and on his journey.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then Jacob saw the ladder, and he is also called Israel; and this Nathanael is an “Israelite indeed in whom is no guile;” therefore when he wondered because the Lord. said to him, “I saw thee under the fig-tree;” did He say to him, “Thou shalt see greater things than these.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so He announced to him Jacob’s dream.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom did He announce it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To him whom He called “an Israelite, in whom was no guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As if He had said, “His dream, by whose name I have called thee, shall be manifested in thee; make no haste to wonder, “thou shalt see greater things than these.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye shall see heaven open, and the Angels of God ascending and descending unto the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what Jacob saw; see why Jacob anointed the stone with oil; see why Jacob prophetically signified and prefigured the Anointed One.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that action was a prophecy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now I know what you are waiting for; I understand what you would hear from me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This too will I briefly declare, as the Lord enableth me; “ascending and descending unto the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How—if they descend to Him, He is here; if they ascend to Him, He is above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if they ascend to Him, and descend toHim, He is at once above and here.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It cannot any way possibly be, that they should ascend to Him, and descend to Him, unless He be both there whither they ascend, and here whither they descend—How do we prove that He is both there, and that He is here?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Paul, who was first Saul, answer us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He found it by experience, when he was first a persecutor, and afterwards became a preacher; first Jacob, afterwards Israel; who was himself too “of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In him let us see Christ above, Christ below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First, the very Voice of the Lord from heaven shows this; “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! had Paul ascended into heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Had Paul so much as cast a stone into heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was persecuting the Christians, binding them, haling them to be put to death, searching them out in every place where they lay hid, when they were found on no consideration sparing them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom the Lord Christ saith, “Saul, Saul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence crieth He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore He is above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore He is below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus have I explained all, though briefly, yet as well as I could to you, Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have ministered to you according to my duty, and now for your duty, do ye think upon the poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us turn to the Lord, etc.
